original_prompt:
# CLAUDE CODE ULTRAWORK — COMPLETE BACKEND BUILD

> **Mode:** ultrathink + yolo (--dangerously-skip-permissions)  
> **Goal:** Production-ready backend that recreates 100% of Chatwoot CE backend functionality, rewritten from scratch in our stack.  
> **Time estimate:** This is a multi-session build. Work through every section sequentially. Do not skip anything.

---

## CONTEXT

You are building the backend for a multi-tenant SaaS customer support platform (like Intercom). You will study Chatwoot's open-source codebase (MIT-licensed CE only, NEVER /enterprise/) as your architectural reference, then rewrite everything from scratch in TypeScript.

**This is not a clone.** You are reading their Ruby/Rails code to understand the COMPLETE business logic, data models, edge cases, and lifecycle rules — then implementing the same functionality in our stack with improvements where appropriate.

**This is a SaaS platform.** We are the platform provider. Businesses sign up, create accounts, connect THEIR channels (their WhatsApp number, their Telegram bot, their email). All channel credentials are per-account data stored encrypted in the database. Nothing customer-specific goes in env vars.

---

## STACK

```
Runtime:        Bun (latest)
Framework:      Elysia (with Eden treaty for e2e type safety)
Database:       Supabase PostgreSQL (direct connection via Drizzle)
ORM:            Drizzle ORM (drizzle-orm + postgres-js driver)
Auth:           Supabase Auth + custom JWT middleware + API tokens
Realtime:       Elysia WebSocket (native Bun WS)
Cache:          Redis via ioredis (hosted Railway)
Queue:          BullMQ on Redis
Storage:        Supabase Storage (S3-compatible)
Email:          Resend SDK
Validation:     Elysia built-in t (TypeBox) schemas
Encryption:     Node crypto AES-256-GCM (for channel credentials)
Monorepo:       Bun workspaces
```

---

## YOUR WORKFLOW FOR EVERY MODULE

1. Browse `https://github.com/chatwoot/chatwoot` — the specific files listed for that module
2. Read the Ruby models, controllers, services, jobs — understand every field, callback, validation, scope, and edge case
3. Rewrite from scratch in TypeScript using our stack
4. Keep the same business logic completeness but simplify Rails-isms (replace ActiveRecord callbacks with explicit service calls, replace Concerns with composition, replace Pundit with guard functions)

**DeepWiki reference:** `https://deepwiki.com/chatwoot/chatwoot` has architecture diagrams and explanations — use it as supplementary reference.

---

## PROJECT STRUCTURE

```
sando/
├── package.json                    # bun workspace root
├── bunfig.toml
├── turbo.json
├── tsconfig.base.json              # shared TS config
│
├── packages/
│   ├── db/                         # Drizzle schema + migrations
│   │   ├── src/
│   │   │   ├── schema/             # one file per domain (see SECTION 1)
│   │   │   ├── client.ts           # drizzle + postgres-js connection
│   │   │   ├── migrate.ts          # run migrations
│   │   │   └── seed.ts             # development seed data
│   │   ├── drizzle/                # generated migration SQL files
│   │   ├── drizzle.config.ts
│   │   └── package.json
│   │
│   ├── core/                       # all business logic (NO HTTP dependency)
│   │   ├── src/
│   │   │   ├── services/           # one file per domain (see SECTION 2)
│   │   │   ├── channels/           # channel handlers (see SECTION 6)
│   │   │   ├── events/             # typed event emitter + types
│   │   │   ├── lib/                # utilities
│   │   │   │   ├── credential-vault.ts
│   │   │   │   ├── pagination.ts
│   │   │   │   ├── errors.ts       # typed error classes
│   │   │   │   └── constants.ts
│   │   │   └── index.ts
│   │   └── package.json
│   │
│   └── queue/                      # BullMQ queues + workers (see SECTION 4)
│       ├── src/
│       │   ├── connection.ts
│       │   ├── queues.ts
│       │   ├── workers/
│       │   └── index.ts
│       └── package.json
│
├── apps/
│   ├── api/                        # Elysia HTTP + WS server
│   │   ├── src/
│   │   │   ├── index.ts            # Elysia entry point
│   │   │   ├── middleware/
│   │   │   ├── routes/
│   │   │   │   ├── v1/             # Application API (see SECTION 3)
│   │   │   │   ├── platform/       # Platform API (super admin)
│   │   │   │   ├── public/         # Public/Widget API
│   │   │   │   └── webhooks/       # Incoming channel webhooks
│   │   │   ├── ws/                 # WebSocket handlers (see SECTION 5)
│   │   │   └── plugins/
│   │   └── package.json
│   │
│   └── worker/                     # standalone BullMQ worker process
│       ├── src/index.ts
│       └── package.json
│
└── .env.example
```

---

# ============================================================
# SECTION 1: DATABASE SCHEMA (packages/db)
# ============================================================

Read these files on GitHub before writing any schema:
- `db/schema.rb` (entire file — this is the source of truth)
- Every model in `app/models/` for validations, enums, scopes
- `app/models/concerns/` for shared behavior

Create Drizzle schema files. Use `snake_case` for DB column names, Drizzle maps to `camelCase` in TS.

**EVERY table gets `account_id` (except `accounts` itself, `users`, `platform_configs`, `super_admins`). This is multi-tenancy.**

## 1.1 accounts.ts

```
accounts
├── id: uuid PK default gen_random_uuid()
├── name: varchar(255) NOT NULL
├── locale: varchar(10) default 'en'
├── domain: varchar(255)
├── support_email: varchar(255)
├── auto_resolve_duration: integer default 0  (minutes, 0=disabled)
├── feature_flags: jsonb default '{}'         (per-account feature toggles)
├── custom_attributes: jsonb default '{}'
├── limits: jsonb default '{}'                (agent_limit, inbox_limit etc based on plan)
├── status: varchar(20) default 'active'      (active, suspended, cancelled)
├── created_at: timestamp default now()
├── updated_at: timestamp default now()
```

Read: `app/models/account.rb` — note the feature flags system, auto_resolve_duration, domain whitelisting, business hours

## 1.2 users.ts

```
users
├── id: uuid PK (maps to Supabase Auth user ID)
├── email: varchar(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE
├── name: varchar(255) NOT NULL
├── display_name: varchar(255)
├── avatar_url: text
├── availability: varchar(20) default 'online' (online, offline, busy)
├── auto_offline: boolean default true         (auto-set offline after inactivity)
├── ui_settings: jsonb default '{}'            (UI preferences)
├── message_signature: text                     (email signature)
├── custom_attributes: jsonb default '{}'
├── created_at / updated_at

account_users (join table — user can belong to multiple accounts)
├── id: uuid PK
├── account_id: uuid FK → accounts NOT NULL
├── user_id: uuid FK → users NOT NULL
├── role: varchar(20) NOT NULL (administrator, agent)
├── availability: varchar(20) default 'online'  (per-account availability!)
├── auto_offline: boolean default true
├── active_at: timestamp                         (last activity)
├── inviter_id: uuid FK → users                 (who invited them)
├── permissions: jsonb default '[]'              (granular permissions array)
├── created_at / updated_at
├── UNIQUE(account_id, user_id)
```

Read: `app/models/user.rb`, `app/models/account_user.rb` — note that availability is tracked PER ACCOUNT (a user can be online in one account, offline in another)

## 1.3 contacts.ts

```
contacts
├── id: uuid PK
├── account_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── name: varchar(255)
├── email: varchar(255)
├── phone_number: varchar(50)
├── identifier: varchar(255)           (external system ID)
├── avatar_url: text
├── additional_attributes: jsonb default '{}'  (social profiles, company info, etc)
├── custom_attributes: jsonb default '{}'
├── contact_type: varchar(20) default 'visitor' (visitor, lead, customer)
├── last_activity_at: timestamp
├── blocked: boolean default false
├── country_code: varchar(10)
├── city: varchar(255)
├── middle_name: varchar(255)
├── last_name: varchar(255)
├── company_name: varchar(255)
├── location: varchar(255)
├── created_at / updated_at
├── UNIQUE(account_id, email) WHERE email IS NOT NULL
├── UNIQUE(account_id, identifier) WHERE identifier IS NOT NULL
├── INDEX(account_id, phone_number)
├── INDEX(account_id, name) for search

contact_inboxes (tracks which contacts came through which channel)
├── id: uuid PK
├── contact_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── inbox_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── source_id: varchar(255) NOT NULL   (channel-specific user ID, e.g. WhatsApp phone, Telegram user_id, FB PSID)
├── hmac_verified: boolean default false (for widget identity verification)
├── pubsub_token: varchar(255)         (for widget real-time subscriptions)
├── created_at / updated_at
├── UNIQUE(inbox_id, source_id)
```

Read: `app/models/contact.rb` — note contact merge logic, custom attributes, contact_type lifecycle (visitor → lead → customer), blocked contacts filtering

## 1.4 inboxes.ts

```
inboxes
├── id: uuid PK
├── account_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── name: varchar(255) NOT NULL
├── channel_type: varchar(100) NOT NULL  (Channel::WebWidget, Channel::Whatsapp, Channel::Telegram, Channel::FacebookPage, Channel::Email, Channel::Sms, Channel::Api, Channel::Instagram)
├── channel_id: uuid NOT NULL            (FK to the specific channel config table)
├── greeting_enabled: boolean default false
├── greeting_message: text
├── enable_auto_assignment: boolean default true
├── out_of_office_message: text
├── working_hours: jsonb                 (array of {day_of_week, open_hour, open_min, close_hour, close_min, closed_all_day})
├── timezone: varchar(100) default 'UTC'
├── enable_email_collect: boolean default true
├── csat_survey_enabled: boolean default false
├── allow_messages_after_resolved: boolean default true
├── lock_to_single_conversation: boolean default false (if true, new messages reopen existing conversation instead of creating new)
├── sender_name_type: varchar(20) default 'friendly' (friendly, professional)
├── business_name: varchar(255)
├── widget_color: varchar(20) default '#1396ED'
├── portal_id: uuid FK → portals         (knowledge base portal)
├── additional_attributes: jsonb default '{}'
├── created_at / updated_at
├── INDEX(account_id)

inbox_members (agents assigned to this inbox)
├── id: uuid PK
├── inbox_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── user_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── created_at / updated_at
├── UNIQUE(inbox_id, user_id)
```

Read: `app/models/inbox.rb` — note working_hours logic (out_of_office checks), the polymorphic channel pattern, auto_assignment settings, lock_to_single_conversation behavior

## 1.5 channels.ts (ALL channel config tables)

**Every credential field marked ▲ must be encrypted at rest using AES-256-GCM via credential-vault.**

```
channel_web_widgets
├── id: uuid PK
├── account_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── website_url: varchar(500)
├── welcome_title: varchar(255)
├── welcome_tagline: varchar(255)
├── feature_flags: jsonb default '{}'    (file_upload, attachments, emoji_picker, etc)
├── reply_time: varchar(20) default 'in_a_few_hours' (in_a_few_minutes, in_a_few_hours, in_a_day)
├── pre_chat_form_enabled: boolean default false
├── pre_chat_form_options: jsonb         ({required: ['email','name'], optional: ['phone']})
├── hmac_token: varchar(255) ▲           (for widget identity verification HMAC)
├── hmac_mandatory: boolean default false
├── continuity_via_email: boolean default true
├── created_at / updated_at

channel_whatsapp
├── id: uuid PK
├── account_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── phone_number: varchar(50) NOT NULL
├── provider: varchar(20) default 'whatsapp_cloud' (whatsapp_cloud, twilio)
├── access_token: text ▲
├── phone_number_id: varchar(100)
├── business_account_id: varchar(100)
├── provider_config: jsonb default '{}'  (webhook_verify_token, api_base etc)
├── message_templates: jsonb default '[]' (cached WA template list)
├── message_templates_last_updated: timestamp
├── created_at / updated_at

channel_telegram_bots
├── id: uuid PK
├── account_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── bot_token: text ▲
├── bot_name: varchar(255)
├── created_at / updated_at

channel_facebook_pages
├── id: uuid PK
├── account_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── page_id: varchar(100) NOT NULL
├── user_access_token: text ▲
├── page_access_token: text ▲
├── instagram_id: varchar(100)          (if IG is connected via same page)
├── created_at / updated_at

channel_email
├── id: uuid PK
├── account_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── email: varchar(255) NOT NULL
├── forward_to_email: varchar(255)      (for email forwarding setup)
├── imap_enabled: boolean default false
├── imap_address: varchar(255)
├── imap_port: integer default 993
├── imap_login: varchar(255)
├── imap_password: text ▲
├── imap_enable_ssl: boolean default true
├── smtp_enabled: boolean default false
├── smtp_address: varchar(255)
├── smtp_port: integer default 587
├── smtp_login: varchar(255)
├── smtp_password: text ▲
├── smtp_domain: varchar(255)
├── smtp_enable_starttls_auto: boolean default true
├── smtp_authentication: varchar(20) default 'login'
├── smtp_openssl_verify_mode: varchar(20) default 'peer'
├── created_at / updated_at

channel_sms
├── id: uuid PK
├── account_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── phone_number: varchar(50) NOT NULL
├── provider: varchar(20) default 'twilio'
├── provider_config: jsonb default '{}'
├── account_sid: varchar(255) ▲
├── auth_token: text ▲
├── created_at / updated_at

channel_api
├── id: uuid PK
├── account_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── identifier: varchar(255)            (public API identifier)
├── webhook_url: varchar(500)
├── hmac_token: text ▲
├── additional_attributes: jsonb default '{}'
├── created_at / updated_at
```

Read: Every file in `app/models/channel/` — Channel::WebWidget, Channel::Whatsapp, Channel::TelegramBot, Channel::FacebookPage, Channel::Email, Channel::Sms, Channel::Api. Understand what config each channel stores and how it's used for sending/receiving.

## 1.6 conversations.ts

```
conversations
├── id: uuid PK
├── account_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── inbox_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── contact_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── contact_inbox_id: uuid FK
├── assignee_id: uuid FK → users         (currently assigned agent)
├── team_id: uuid FK → teams
├── campaign_id: uuid FK → campaigns
├── display_id: serial                    (auto-incrementing per account! e.g. #1, #2, #3)
├── status: integer NOT NULL default 0    (0=open, 1=resolved, 2=pending, 3=snoozed, 4=abandoned)
├── priority: varchar(20)                 (null, urgent, high, medium, low)
├── snoozed_until: timestamp
├── first_reply_created_at: timestamp     (tracks time to first agent reply)
├── waiting_since: timestamp              (set when customer sends message, cleared when agent replies)
├── last_activity_at: timestamp NOT NULL default now()
├── agent_last_seen_at: timestamp
├── contact_last_seen_at: timestamp
├── uuid: varchar(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE default gen_random_uuid()  (public identifier)
├── identifier: varchar(255)              (external ticket ID)
├── additional_attributes: jsonb default '{}'  (browser_info, referer, initiated_at etc)
├── custom_attributes: jsonb default '{}'
├── cached_label_list: text[]             (denormalized label names for fast filtering)
├── muted: boolean default false          (muted conversations don't reopen on new messages)
├── created_at / updated_at
├── INDEX(account_id, display_id) UNIQUE
├── INDEX(account_id, status)
├── INDEX(account_id, inbox_id)
├── INDEX(account_id, assignee_id)
├── INDEX(account_id, team_id)
├── INDEX(account_id, priority)
├── INDEX(contact_id)
```

Read: `app/models/conversation.rb` — THIS IS THE MOST IMPORTANT MODEL. Study:
- The full status state machine (open ↔ resolved ↔ pending ↔ snoozed)
- `display_id` auto-increment per account (not global!)
- `waiting_since` logic (set on incoming message, cleared on outgoing)
- `first_reply_created_at` tracking
- Auto-resolve logic (resolved after X minutes of inactivity)
- Mute/unmute behavior
- All the after_commit callbacks: notify_participants, create_activity, dispatch_events
- `activity_message_handler.rb` — creates system messages on status changes, assignment changes, label changes
- `sla_handler.rb` if present
- Conversation reopening rules (new incoming message reopens resolved conversations unless muted)
- `lock_to_single_conversation` inbox setting

## 1.7 messages.ts

```
messages
├── id: uuid PK
├── account_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── conversation_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── inbox_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── sender_type: varchar(50)              ('User' for agents, 'Contact' for customers, 'AgentBot' for bots)
├── sender_id: uuid                       (polymorphic — user_id or contact_id or bot_id)
├── message_type: integer NOT NULL         (0=incoming, 1=outgoing, 2=activity, 3=template)
├── content_type: varchar(50) default 'text' (text, input_select, cards, form, article, input_email, input_csat, integrations, sticker)
├── content: text                          (message text/HTML)
├── content_attributes: jsonb default '{}' (channel-specific metadata, e.g. email headers, WA template params)
├── private: boolean default false         (internal notes — not visible to customer)
├── status: varchar(20) default 'sent'     (sent, delivered, read, failed)
├── source_id: varchar(255)                (external message ID from channel)
├── external_source_ids: jsonb default '{}' (for tracking across systems)
├── sentiment: jsonb                        (AI-derived sentiment data)
├── processed_message_content: text         (cleaned/processed version)
├── additional_attributes: jsonb default '{}' (in_reply_to, email metadata, etc)
├── created_at / updated_at
├── INDEX(conversation_id, created_at)
├── INDEX(account_id, inbox_id)
├── INDEX(source_id)

attachments
├── id: uuid PK
├── account_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── message_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── file_type: varchar(20) NOT NULL       (image, audio, video, file, location, contact, fallback)
├── file_url: text                         (Supabase Storage URL)
├── external_url: text                     (if hosted externally)
├── file_name: varchar(500)
├── file_size: bigint                      (bytes)
├── width: integer
├── height: integer
├── data_url: text                         (thumbnail/preview for images)
├── thumb_url: text
├── coordinates_lat: decimal
├── coordinates_long: decimal
├── fallback_title: varchar(500)
├── fallback_url: text
├── extension: varchar(50)
├── created_at / updated_at
```

Read: `app/models/message.rb` — CRITICAL. Study:
- The after_commit callback chain: `reopen_conversation`, `notify_via_mail`, `dispatch_create_events`, `send_reply`, `execute_after_create_commit_callbacks`
- `reopen_conversation` — incoming messages reopen resolved/snoozed conversations (unless muted). Bot-assigned conversations go to pending, not open.
- Rate limiting: max 50 messages per conversation per minute
- Activity messages (message_type=2): system-generated messages for status changes, assignment changes, label changes, etc.
- `content_type` variations and what `content_attributes` looks like for each
- `private` messages (internal notes) — never sent to customer channel, never trigger customer notifications
- `waiting_since` update on the conversation when an incoming message arrives

## 1.8 teams.ts

```
teams
├── id: uuid PK
├── account_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── name: varchar(255) NOT NULL
├── description: text
├── allow_auto_assign: boolean default true
├── created_at / updated_at
├── UNIQUE(account_id, name)

team_members
├── id: uuid PK
├── team_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── user_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── created_at / updated_at
├── UNIQUE(team_id, user_id)
```

## 1.9 labels.ts

```
labels
├── id: uuid PK
├── account_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── title: varchar(255) NOT NULL
├── description: text
├── color: varchar(20) default '#1396ED'
├── show_on_sidebar: boolean default true
├── created_at / updated_at
├── UNIQUE(account_id, title)

conversation_labels (join table — many-to-many)
├── id: uuid PK
├── conversation_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── label_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── created_at
├── UNIQUE(conversation_id, label_id)
```

## 1.10 canned_responses.ts

```
canned_responses
├── id: uuid PK
├── account_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── short_code: varchar(100) NOT NULL     (e.g. "greeting" — agent types /greeting to insert)
├── content: text NOT NULL                (the response template, supports {{variables}})
├── created_at / updated_at
├── UNIQUE(account_id, short_code)
```

## 1.11 automation_rules.ts

```
automation_rules
├── id: uuid PK
├── account_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── name: varchar(255) NOT NULL
├── description: text
├── event_name: varchar(100) NOT NULL     (conversation_created, conversation_updated, message_created)
├── conditions: jsonb NOT NULL            (array of {attribute, filter_operator, values, query_operator})
├── actions: jsonb NOT NULL               (array of {action_name, action_params})
├── active: boolean default true
├── created_at / updated_at

-- Conditions example:
-- [{"attribute": "status", "filter_operator": "equal_to", "values": ["open"], "query_operator": "AND"},
--  {"attribute": "inbox_id", "filter_operator": "equal_to", "values": ["uuid-here"], "query_operator": null}]
--
-- Actions example:
-- [{"action_name": "assign_team", "action_params": ["team-uuid"]},
--  {"action_name": "add_label", "action_params": ["urgent"]},
--  {"action_name": "send_message", "action_params": ["We received your message!"]}]

-- Supported action_name values:
-- assign_agent, assign_team, add_label, remove_label, send_email_to_team,
-- send_message, mute_conversation, snooze_conversation, resolve_conversation,
-- change_status, change_priority, send_webhook_event, send_email_transcript
```

Read: `app/models/automation_rule.rb`, `app/services/automation_rules/` — understand the full condition matching engine and all action executors

## 1.12 macros.ts

```
macros
├── id: uuid PK
├── account_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── name: varchar(255) NOT NULL
├── visibility: varchar(20) default 'personal' (personal, global)
├── created_by_id: uuid FK → users
├── actions: jsonb NOT NULL               (same format as automation_rule actions)
├── created_at / updated_at
```

## 1.13 webhooks.ts

```
webhooks
├── id: uuid PK
├── account_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── url: varchar(500) NOT NULL
├── subscriptions: text[] NOT NULL default '{}' (event types: conversation_created, conversation_status_changed, conversation_updated, contact_created, contact_updated, message_created, message_updated, webwidget_triggered)
├── hmac_token: varchar(255) ▲
├── created_at / updated_at
```

## 1.14 notifications.ts

```
notifications
├── id: uuid PK
├── account_id: uuid FK NOT NULL
├── user_id: uuid FK NOT NULL             (recipient agent)
├── notification_type: varchar(50) NOT NULL (conversation_creation, conversation_assignment, assigned_conversation_new_message, participating_conversation_new_message, conversation_mention, sla_missed_first_response, sla_missed_next_response, sla_missed_resolution)
├── primary_actor_type: varchar(50)        (Conversation, Message)
├── primary_actor_id: uuid
├── secondary_actor_type: varchar(50)      (User, Contact)
├── secondary_actor_id: uuid
├── read_at: timestamp                     (null = unread)
├── snoozed_until: timestamp
├── last_activity_at: timestamp
├── meta: jsonb default '{}'
├── created_at / updated_at
├── INDEX(account_id, user_id, read_at)

notification_subscriptions (push notification device tokens)
```

# ============================================================
# SECTION 2: CORE SERVICES (packages/core)
# ============================================================

Each service is a module of pure functions. No HTTP dependency. Takes db client + params, returns typed results. Every function that touches tenant data takes `accountId` as first parameter.

## 2.1 credential-vault.ts

```typescript
// AES-256-GCM encryption for channel credentials
// Uses ENCRYPTION_KEY from env (32 bytes)
export function encrypt(plaintext: string): string // returns "iv:authTag:ciphertext" base64
export function decrypt(encrypted: string): string
```

This MUST be fully implemented. Used by every channel config read/write.

## 2.2 account.service.ts

Read: `app/models/account.rb`, `app/controllers/api/v1/accounts_controller.rb`

```
create(params) → account
get(id) → account + subscription info
update(id, params) → account
updateFeatureFlags(id, flags) → account
getAccountLimits(id) → {agents: current/max, inboxes: current/max, ...}
```

## 2.3 auth.service.ts

Read: `app/controllers/concerns/authentication.rb`, `app/models/access_token.rb`

Three auth methods:
1. **Supabase Auth JWT** — for dashboard users. Verify JWT, look up account_user, check role.
2. **User API Token** — `api_access_token` header. Lookup in access_tokens table.
3. **Platform API Token** — for super-admin/platform operations.
4. **Bot API Token** — for agent_bot requests.

```
verifyJWT(token) → { user, accountUser }
verifyApiToken(token) → { owner, ownerType }
generateApiToken(ownerType, ownerId) → token
revokeApiToken(tokenId)
checkPermission(accountUser, action, resource) → boolean
```

## 2.4 contact.service.ts

Read: `app/models/contact.rb`, `app/services/contacts/`, `app/controllers/api/v1/accounts/contacts_controller.rb`

```
list(accountId, filters, pagination) → { contacts, meta }
get(accountId, contactId) → contact with contact_inboxes
create(accountId, params) → contact
update(accountId, contactId, params) → contact
delete(accountId, contactId) → void
search(accountId, query, page) → contacts   // search by name, email, phone, identifier
merge(accountId, baseContactId, mergeContactId) → merged contact  // IMPORTANT: merge all conversations, contact_inboxes, messages
getConversations(accountId, contactId) → conversations
filter(accountId, filterPayload) → contacts  // advanced filtering
importContacts(accountId, csvData) → import job
```

**Contact merge logic is complex:** When merging contact B into contact A:
- Move all conversations from B to A
- Move all contact_inboxes from B to A (skip duplicates)
- Update all messages where sender is B to point to A
- Merge custom_attributes (A takes priority)
- Delete contact B

## 2.5 conversation.service.ts — THE MOST CRITICAL SERVICE

Read: `app/models/conversation.rb`, `app/controllers/api/v1/accounts/conversations_controller.rb`, `app/services/conversations/`, `app/models/concerns/activity_message_handler.rb`, `app/finders/conversation_finder.rb`

```
list(accountId, params) → { conversations, meta }
  // params: status, assignee_type (me/unassigned/all), inbox_id, team_id, label, page
  // assignee_type 'me' = assigned to current user
  // assignee_type 'unassigned' = assignee_id IS NULL
  // sort by last_activity_at DESC

get(accountId, conversationId) → conversation with messages, contact, inbox, assignee, team, labels, participants

create(accountId, params) → conversation
  // params: inbox_id, contact_id, message (initial message), assignee_id, team_id, status
  // Auto-assign if inbox.enable_auto_assignment is true
  // If inbox has an agent_bot, set assignee to bot, status to pending
  // Create initial message if provided
  // dispatch conversation_created event

toggleStatus(accountId, conversationId, status) → conversation
  // open ↔ resolved ↔ pending ↔ snoozed
  // Create activity message: "Conversation was resolved by Agent Name"
  // If resolving: record reporting_event (conversation_resolved, time to resolution)
  // If reopening from resolved: only if allow_messages_after_resolved
  // dispatch conversation_status_changed event

assignAgent(accountId, conversationId, agentId) → conversation
  // Create activity message: "Conversation was assigned to Agent Name by Assigner Name"
  // Notify new assignee
  // dispatch conversation_assignee_changed event

assignTeam(accountId, conversationId, teamId) → conversation
  // Create activity message: "Conversation was assigned to Team Name"
  // dispatch conversation_team_changed event

updatePriority(accountId, conversationId, priority) → conversation
  // Create activity message: "Priority was changed to High by Agent Name"

addLabels(accountId, conversationId, labelIds) → conversation
  // Update cached_label_list
  // Create activity message for each label added
  // dispatch conversation_updated event

removeLabels(accountId, conversationId, labelIds) → conversation

mute(accountId, conversationId) → void
  // Muted conversations do NOT reopen on new incoming messages

unmute(accountId, conversationId) → void

snooze(accountId, conversationId, snoozedUntil: Date) → conversation
  // Set status to snoozed, set snoozed_until
  // Schedule a job to reopen at snoozed_until

addParticipant(accountId, conversationId, userId) → void
removeParticipant(accountId, conversationId, userId) → void

getParticipants(accountId, conversationId) → users

filter(accountId, filterPayload) → { conversations, meta }
  // Complex multi-attribute filtering with AND/OR logic
  // Supported attributes: status, assignee_id, team_id, inbox_id, label, priority,
  //   contact.name, contact.email, contact.phone, contact.identifier,
  //   created_at, last_activity_at, browser_language, country_code, referer
  // Operators: equal_to, not_equal_to, contains, does_not_contain,
  //   is_present, is_not_present, is_greater_than, is_less_than, days_before

autoResolve(accountId) → resolved count
  // Called by scheduled job
  // Resolve conversations where:
  //   status = open or pending
  //   last_activity_at < (now - account.auto_resolve_duration)
  //   auto_resolve_duration > 0
  // Create activity message: "Conversation was auto-resolved due to inactivity"
```

Read: `app/finders/conversation_finder.rb` for the EXACT filtering/sorting logic. It's complex.

## 2.6 message.service.ts

Read: `app/models/message.rb`, `app/controllers/api/v1/accounts/conversations/messages_controller.rb`, `app/builders/messages/`

```
list(accountId, conversationId, params) → { messages, meta }
  // pagination: before (message_id), after (message_id) — cursor-based
  // Include attachments, sender info

create(accountId, conversationId, params) → message
  // params: content, message_type, content_type, sender_type, sender_id, private, content_attributes, attachments, template_params, echo_id (client dedup)
  //
  // CRITICAL LIFECYCLE ON CREATE:
  // 1. Rate limit check: max 50 messages per conversation per minute
  // 2. If incoming (from customer):
  //    a. Set conversation.waiting_since = now
  //    b. If conversation is resolved and NOT muted → reopen to 'open'
  //    c. If conversation is snoozed → reopen to 'open'
  //    d. If conversation assigned to bot → set status to 'pending'
  //    e. Update contact.last_activity_at
  // 3. If outgoing (from agent):
  //    a. Clear conversation.waiting_since
  //    b. Track first_reply_created_at if not already set
  //    c. Record reporting_event (first_response or reply_time)
  //    d. Enqueue channel delivery job (send to WhatsApp/Telegram/etc)
  // 4. If NOT private:
  //    a. Notify conversation participants
  //    b. Dispatch webhook events
  //    c. Trigger automation rules
  // 5. If private (internal note):
  //    a. Notify mentioned agents only
  //    b. Do NOT send to channel, do NOT notify customer
  // 6. Update conversation.last_activity_at
  // 7. Broadcast via WebSocket

update(accountId, messageId, params) → message
  // Only content and submitted_values can be updated after creation

delete(accountId, messageId) → void
  // Soft-delete or mark as deleted

getAttachments(accountId, messageId) → attachments
```

## 2.10 automation.service.ts

Read: `app/models/automation_rule.rb`, `app/services/automation_rules/action_service.rb`, `app/services/automation_rules/conditions_filter_service.rb`

```
processEvent(accountId, eventName, eventData) → void
  // 1. Fetch all active automation rules for this account matching eventName
  // 2. For each rule, evaluate conditions against eventData
  // 3. If conditions match, execute actions in order
  //
  // Condition matching supports:
  //   Attributes: status, priority, assignee_id, team_id, inbox_id, label_list,
  //     mail_subject, referer, city, country, company_name, browser_language,
  //     message_type, content (regex match)
  //   Operators: equal_to, not_equal_to, contains, does_not_contain,
  //     is_present, is_not_present
  //
  // Action executors:
  //   assign_agent(userId) → update conversation assignee
  //   assign_team(teamId) → update conversation team
  //   add_label(label) → add label to conversation
  //   remove_label(label) → remove label
  //   send_message(content) → create outgoing message (activity type)
  //   send_email_to_team(teamId, subject, body) → enqueue email job
  //   mute_conversation → mute
  //   snooze_conversation → snooze (until specified time or default)
  //   resolve_conversation → resolve
  //   change_status(status) → change conversation status
  //   change_priority(priority) → change priority
  //   send_webhook_event(url) → enqueue webhook delivery
  //   send_email_transcript(email) → send conversation transcript via email

executeMacro(accountId, macroId, conversationIds, userId) → void
  // Execute a macro's actions on one or more conversations
```

## 2.16 Event System

```typescript
// events/types.ts — define ALL events
type AppEvents = {
  'conversation.created': { conversation: Conversation; account_id: string }
  'conversation.status_changed': { conversation: Conversation; old_status: string; changed_by: User | null }
  'conversation.assignee_changed': { conversation: Conversation; old_assignee_id: string | null; changed_by: User | null }
  'conversation.team_changed': { conversation: Conversation; old_team_id: string | null }
  'conversation.priority_changed': { conversation: Conversation; old_priority: string | null }
  'conversation.label_added': { conversation: Conversation; label: Label }
  'conversation.label_removed': { conversation: Conversation; label: Label }
  'conversation.contact_changed': { conversation: Conversation }
  'conversation.muted': { conversation: Conversation }
  'conversation.unmuted': { conversation: Conversation }
  'message.created': { message: Message; conversation: Conversation }
  'message.updated': { message: Message; conversation: Conversation }
  'contact.created': { contact: Contact; account_id: string }
  'contact.updated': { contact: Contact }
  'contact.deleted': { contact: Contact }
  'inbox.created': { inbox: Inbox }
  'webwidget.triggered': { contact: Contact; event: any; account_id: string }
}

// events/emitter.ts — typed EventEmitter
// All services emit events after state changes
// Listeners:
//   WebSocketBroadcaster — pushes to connected dashboard clients
//   WebhookDispatcher — enqueues webhook delivery jobs
//   NotificationCreator — creates notification records
//   AutomationProcessor — evaluates automation rules
//   ReportingRecorder — records reporting events
```

# ============================================================
# SECTION 3: API LAYER (apps/api)
# ============================================================

Read: `config/routes.rb`, `app/controllers/api/v1/accounts/` (every controller), `swagger/paths/`

## 3.1 Middleware

```
auth.ts
  - Parse Authorization header
  - If Bearer token → verify Supabase JWT → load user + account_users
  - If api_access_token → lookup in access_tokens → load owner
  - If user_token (for platform API) → verify platform token
  - Attach to Elysia context: { user, accountUser, tokenType }

account-scope.ts
  - Extract :account_id from URL params
  - Verify current user has account_user record for this account
  - Verify account is active (not suspended/cancelled)
  - Check subscription limits (e.g., reject if plan expired)
  - Attach account to context

rbac.ts (role-based access guard)
  - Use Elysia .guard() pattern
  - Check accountUser.role against required role
  - administrator: all access
  - agent: limited (can't manage inboxes, teams, billing, etc.)

rate-limit.ts
  - Redis sliding window rate limiter
  - Different limits per endpoint type:
    - API: 300 req/min per user
    - Widget: 60 req/min per IP
    - Webhooks: 100 req/min per account
```

# ============================================================
# SECTION 4: QUEUE / WORKERS (packages/queue)
# ============================================================

Read: `app/jobs/` (ALL job files), `app/jobs/webhooks/`, `app/jobs/inboxes/`

BullMQ queues:

```
channel-delivery        — send outgoing messages to channels (WhatsApp, Telegram, Email, etc)
webhook-delivery        — POST to customer-registered webhook URLs
email-notification      — send email notifications to agents
email-inbound           — process inbound emails
automation-executor     — run automation rule actions
campaign-trigger        — send campaign messages
reporting-rollup        — periodic metrics aggregation
conversation-resolver   — auto-resolve stale conversations (scheduled, runs every 15 min)
snoozed-reopener        — reopen snoozed conversations at snoozed_until time
contact-import          — process CSV contact imports
cleanup                 — delete old notifications, reporting data, etc (scheduled daily)
```

Each worker:
- Is idempotent (safe to retry)
- Has exponential backoff retry (3 attempts default)
- Uses Redis mutex locks for conversation-scoped operations (prevent race conditions on same conversation)
- Logs failures for debugging

**Critical: channel-delivery worker**
```
For each message to deliver:
1. Load inbox + channel config
2. Decrypt credentials
3. Based on channel_type, call the appropriate channel handler's sendMessage()
4. Update message.source_id with external message ID
5. Update message.status to 'delivered' or 'failed'
6. On failure: retry with backoff, after final failure set status='failed'
```

**Critical: conversation-resolver worker (scheduled)**
```
Every 15 minutes:
1. For each account with auto_resolve_duration > 0:
2. Find conversations where status IN (open, pending)
   AND last_activity_at < (now - auto_resolve_duration minutes)
3. Resolve each, creating activity message
```

# ============================================================
# SECTION 5: WEBSOCKET LAYER (apps/api/src/ws/)
# ============================================================

Read: `app/channels/`, `app/dispatchers/`, `app/listeners/action_cable_listener.rb`

Implement WebSocket at `/cable` using Elysia's native WS:

```
Connection:
  - Authenticate via token query param (?token=xxx)
  - Track connected users in Redis SET: "presence:#{account_id}"
  - On disconnect: remove from presence set, broadcast agent offline

Subscriptions (per connection):
  - Account-level events (conversation list updates)
  - Conversation-specific events (when viewing a conversation)

Events to broadcast:
  conversation.created       → all connected agents in account
  conversation.status_changed → all connected agents in account
  conversation.assignee_changed → old assignee + new assignee + account
  conversation.typing_on/off → participants in that conversation
  conversation.contact_changed → participants
  message.created            → all participants + agents viewing this conversation
  message.updated            → same
  contact.created/updated    → account-level
  notification.created       → specific user
  presence.update            → account-level (agent went online/offline)
  conversation.read          → conversation participants (agent saw messages)
  CSAT.submitted             → conversation assignee

Message format: { event: string, data: any, account_id: string, conversation_id?: string }
```

Read: `app/listeners/action_cable_listener.rb` to see EXACTLY what events get broadcast and their payload shape.

# ============================================================
# SECTION 6: CHANNEL INTEGRATION LAYER (packages/core/src/channels/)
# ============================================================

Read: ALL files in `app/services/telegram/`, `app/services/whatsapp/`, `app/services/facebook/`, `app/services/instagram/`, `app/services/twilio/`, `app/services/email_templates/`

Also read: ALL files in `app/builders/messages/` (message builders per channel)

```typescript
interface ChannelHandler {
  // Register webhook with external service (e.g., Telegram setWebhook)
  connect(inbox: Inbox, config: ChannelConfig): Promise<{ webhookUrl: string }>
  
  // Process incoming webhook payload → create contact + message
  processIncoming(inboxId: string, payload: unknown): Promise<{ 
    contact: Contact | null
    contactInbox: ContactInbox
    conversation: Conversation
    message: Message
  }>
  
  // Send outgoing message to channel
  sendMessage(inbox: Inbox, config: ChannelConfig, message: Message, contact: Contact): Promise<{ 
    externalId: string
    deliveryStatus: string 
  }>
  
  // Handle delivery/read receipts
  processStatusUpdate?(inboxId: string, payload: unknown): Promise<void>
  
  // Disconnect (remove webhook)
  disconnect(inbox: Inbox, config: ChannelConfig): Promise<void>
}
```

**Implement FULLY for:** Web Widget, Email, Telegram, API Channel  
**Implement with correct interfaces + basic send for:** WhatsApp Cloud API, Facebook Messenger, Instagram, SMS (Twilio)

For each channel's `processIncoming`, the flow is:
1. Parse channel-specific webhook payload
2. Find or create Contact (by channel-specific ID → contact_inbox.source_id)
3. If new contact, fetch profile from channel API (name, avatar, etc.)
4. Find or create Conversation (lookup existing open conversation for this contact+inbox, or create new)
5. Create Message with normalized content + attachments
6. Handle special message types (reactions, story replies, stickers, location, etc.)

Read the message builder files in Chatwoot to understand the exact normalization for each channel.

# ============================================================
# EXECUTION RULES
# ============================================================

1. **Do not ask questions.** Make the best decision and document it in a code comment.
2. **Write COMPLETE, WORKING code.** Every service, every endpoint, every worker — fully implemented. The only exceptions: WhatsApp/Facebook/Instagram/SMS channel *senders* can have basic implementations (correct API calls but not every edge case). Everything else must be production-complete.
3. **Browse Chatwoot source for EVERY module before implementing.** Don't guess. Read the Ruby code, understand the logic, then translate to TypeScript.
4. **Multi-tenancy is sacred.** Every DB query filters by account_id. No exceptions. No "oops I forgot the WHERE clause."
5. **Encrypt all channel credentials.** credential-vault.ts is not optional.
6. **Type everything.** No `any`. Use Drizzle inferred types. Use Elysia's TypeBox schemas for API validation.
7. **Test as you go.** After each section, run `bun run typecheck`. After the API is built, verify the server starts with `bun run dev`.
8. **Commit after each major section.** Use descriptive commit messages.
9. **If Chatwoot has a pattern that's overcomplicated for Rails reasons, simplify it.** But never lose the underlying business logic.
10. **When in doubt, match Chatwoot's behavior exactly.** We want API-level compatibility so any frontend built for Chatwoot could work with our backend with minimal changes.
